message:
feat(storage): tick 175 highest-volume ingest writes route through send_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Continues the ft-xbnl0.2.3 cx-first rollout that began in tick 170 and
proceeded cluster-by-cluster through tick 174 (see d2d84c4a, 5a658160,
2fdd7175, c441bddb, 20f5fb61, de2e98d5).

Previously these _with_cx wrappers performed the pre-flight cx.checkpoint()
and then delegated to the non-cx siblings, which meant the actual mpsc
enqueue to the writer thread bypassed cx-aware backpressure. This tick
inlines the body so the WriteCommand is enqueued via
`self.write_tx.send_with_cx(cx, …)` instead of the plain `.send(…)` path,
giving cancel/deadline propagation a second observation point between the
checkpoint and the writer-thread response wait.

Wrappers converted:
- append_segment_with_cx — the highest-volume per-pane ingest path.
  Segment appends dominate write traffic, so routing this through
  send_with_cx is the biggest single-callsite win in this series.
- record_gap_with_cx — called from ingest detector paths where cx
  propagation from upstream observers is already common.
- record_maintenance_with_cx and record_usage_metric_with_cx — lower
  volume but on the same cluster; converted together to keep the
  WriteCommand enqueue policy uniform.

Mechanically identical to prior ticks in this series: build the oneshot,
send_with_cx the WriteCommand variant, then await recv_writer_response.
No schema, behavior, or public-API changes; feature-gated on
`asupersync-runtime` as before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
